idea-plugin.name: AzerothJS
idea-plugin.description:
Official language support for AzerothJS - the fine-grained fullstack framework. Full intelligence for .azeroth single-file components, powered by the framework's own compiler.

The .ts ⇆ .azeroth boundary, dissolved: go-to-definition, find usages, and safe rename work in both directions between TypeScript files and components - one codebase, not two worlds. The IDE's unused-symbol analysis sees component usages too, so a .ts export used only from markup is never flagged.

  • Native .azeroth language: syntax highlighting, correct bracket matching (a real lexer - braces inside strings and template interpolations never break the pairing), matching-tag highlight, and the AzerothJS file icon.
  • Compiler-aware intelligence over LSP: completion with auto-imports, hover documentation for every AzerothJS keyword and built-in component, real type diagnostics, navigation, rename, signature help, formatting, and semantic highlighting with a distinct color for reactive names.
  • Zero config: open a project, open a component, everything works.

Requirements: WebStorm / a paid JetBrains IDE 2026.1+ (the LSP API) and Node.js. Node is auto-detected from PATH and common version managers (nvm, fnm, volta, Homebrew); set an explicit path under Settings → Languages & Frameworks → AzerothJS if needed.

New to AzerothJS? npm create azeroth@latest scaffolds a working app in one command.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.0-beta - the framework's first 1.0 beta, and the plugin grows with it:

  • The AzerothJS file icon for .azeroth files (light and dark).
  • Find Usages and unused-symbol analysis now see component usages of .ts exports.
  • Language server updated to the 1.0 compiler: keyword hover docs, auto-imports, cross-file rename hardened.
